meta.title: Tono City Akabane Ski Area
meta.description: Folktale country, rope-tow challenge—retro hill at ¥1,400 a day.

hero.eyebrow: Tono, Iwate · Folktale country · Closest snow from Sanriku
hero.title:
Tono City Akabane
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Folktale country, rope-tow challenge—retro hill at ¥1,400 a day.

live.subtitle: Check Tono City official info & on-site notices
live.updatedAt: Low-snow seasons may delay or cancel opening—confirm before you go

lift1.label: Lifts
lift1.note: Rope tow only · no chairlifts · 2–3 operating

lift2.label: Courses
lift2.note: Kamoshika & Kabe runs · kids sled area

snow.label: Snow
snow.note: High shortage risk in recent years · check official updates

sanctuary.title:
Showa-era rope tows,
snow hill in folktale country.
sanctuary.lead: A rare municipal hill with three Anvár rope tows only—no chairlifts. About 45 km from the Kamaishi coast, the closest snow play spot from the sea. Compact and easy to pair with Tono Jingisukan culture and folktale sightseeing.
sanctuary.noticeBody: Warming winters have brought seasons with delayed opening or sharply fewer operating days—including 2023–24, when opening was still uncertain by mid-January. There is no live snow API; check Tono City notices, on-site boards, and weather forecasts before every trip.
sanctuary.imgAlt: lp-mock-tono-akabane-lounge—retro rope-tow queue scene at the lodge

primary.title: Kamoshika forest run & steep Kabe
primary.lead: From gentle beginner slopes to the forest Kamoshika course and advanced Kabe (max ~30°, ~500–600 m). A dedicated kids sled area too—without chairlifts, short laps make it ideal for repetition training.
primary.imgAlt: lp-mock-tono-akabane-hero—panorama of rope tows and three courses

secondary.title: Gear rental yes, wear rental no
secondary.lead: Ski or snowboard set ¥1,000/day each. No wear, sled, goggle, or glove rental—travel light and plan ahead. Lodge cafeteria open weekends only (Sat–Sun 11:00–14:00).

bundle.title:
Adult ¥1,400/day,
single ride ¥60.
bundle.lead: Single ride ¥60, 12-ride ¥600, morning/afternoon ¥800 each, day pass ¥1,400. Weekday night skiing (17:00–20:45) ¥1,000. Season pass ¥10,500. Preschool lift free. Ski/snowboard set rental ¥1,000 each—no clothing rental.
bundle.imgAlt: lp-mock-tono-akabane-wakasagi—bucket Jingisukan snow picnic on the slopes

wellness.title:
Then Jingisukan
and hot springs.
wellness.lead: On-hill food is weekends only. For real Tono flavor: bucket Jingisukan at Jingisukan Anbe and lamb at Tono Meat Center. Soak at Odoro Onsen Ten-no-Yu, then folktale stops at Denshoen (Kappa Pool) and Michi-no-Eki Tono Kaze-no-Oka.
wellness.leadStation: ~2 km from Tono-Sumata IC—~45 km from central Kamaishi, the easiest snow hill from the Sanriku coast.

tour.title:
Slot half a day
into folktale & food.
tour.lead: Base in Morioka or Kamaishi: morning rope-tow laps at Akabane, afternoon bucket Jingisukan at Anbe, bath at Ten-no-Yu—a Tono snow-culture loop beyond lift tickets alone.

01.title: Morioka Station · Kamaishi area
01.body: ~1 hr drive from Morioka · ~45 km from Kamaishi · ~2 km from Sumata IC

02.title: Arrive at slopes
02.body: Confirm snow & operating tows on site · bring your own wear

03.title: 2–3 hours skiing
03.body: Day pass ¥1,400 · single ¥60 · conquer the rope tows

05.title: Odoro Onsen Ten-no-Yu
05.body: Pair with Denshoen & Kaze-no-Oka folktale route

ecosystem.title: Three rope tows & compact courses
ecosystem.body: No chairlifts or gondolas—only three Anvár rope tows you ride by holding a bar against your back or gripping a handle. Kamoshika, Kabe, and a kids sled area—a small hill loved locally for Showa retro rarity and a ¥1,400 day pass.

access.title: ~2 km from Sumata IC · ~45 km from Kamaishi
access.body: ~2 km from Kamaishi Expressway Tono-Sumata IC. ~44–45 km (~55 min–1 hr drive) from central Kamaishi. ~1 hr from Morioka via national and prefectural roads—the closest snow hill from the Sanriku coast.

green.title: Folktale in summer, rope tows in winter
green.body: As snowless seasons increase, value comes from short-window ski days plus year-round Tono tourism—Denshoen, Kaze-no-Oka, and Jingisukan. Winter: retro rope-tow experience. Summer: Kappa Pool and satoyama culture—area spending, not lift tickets alone.

access.note: ~2 km from Tono-Sumata IC · ~45 km from Kamaishi center · no wear rental · cafeteria weekends only
access.places: 岩手県Iwate遠野市Tono上郷町Kamigo平倉Hirakura41-47
access.rentacarEyebrow: ロープトゥ×ジンギスカン×民話
access.rentacarLink: 盛岡駅でレンタカー予約
access.rentacarNote: スカイチケット（外部サイト）
access.rentacarHint: 遠野市赤羽根スキー場周辺のドライブ向け

nearbyFood.notice: Hours and reservations are on each venue's official site.
nearbyFood.introLead: From the weekend-only lodge cafeteria to signature Jingisukan and local food—four stops that fit a half-day ski plus folktale plan.
nearbyFood.introBody: Lunch on the hill or at Anbe; evening at onsen town or Kaze-no-Oka.
nearbyFood.spotsLead: From the lodge into Tono town, in route order.

slope.title: Akabane lodge cafeteria
slope.lead: Open Sat–Sun 11:00–14:00 only. No food on weekdays or during night skiing—bring snacks or onigiri. Note: no toilets inside the lodge; use the separate building.

prison.title: Jingisukan Anbe
prison.lead: Famous for bucket Jingisukan—a Tono signature. Perfect after skiing or as a snow picnic partner. Reservations required; busy in winter.

soba.title: Tono Meat Center
soba.lead: Local lamb at the municipal meat center—stock up for DIY Jingisukan or taste Tono's sheep culture. Core of a lamb-focused Tono food loop with Anbe.

crab.title: Michi-no-Eki Tono Kaze-no-Oka
crab.lead: Tono produce, light meals, and travel info. Pair satoyama views and folktale monuments with a post-ski break and souvenir stop—gateway to Denshoen (Kappa Pool).

nearbyOnsen.notice: Fees and hours are on each facility's official site.
nearbyOnsen.introLead: Hot springs within easy drive of Akabane—warm rope-tow-chilled legs in satoyama baths is this area's signature loop.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Odoro Onsen near folktale country and Takamuro Suiko-en with lodging—two curated picks.
nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Recovery and lodging bases after skiing.

kohanso.title: Odoro Onsen Ten-no-Yu
kohanso.lead: Satoyama hot spring in Tono. Clear, soothing water for cold legs—hub of a folktale route with Denshoen and Kaze-no-Oka. The default recovery stop after skiing.

kanko.title: Takamuro Suiko-en
kanko.lead: Lodging with onsen in Tono city. Quiet satoyama setting for an overnight, then onward to Morioka or Kamaishi—a two-day ski + bath + folktale base.

hero.title:
Sleep in Tono,
or hub at Morioka.
hero.tagline: Folktale town stay vs regional loop

corridorStay.notice: Rooms, rates, and shuttles are on each property's booking site.
corridorStay.introLead: Stay in Tono and loop Akabane, Denshoen, and Anbe—or use Morioka Station as your Iwate hub. Choose by what your trip is about.

kohanso.tag: ~2 km from Sumata IC · folktale · Jingisukan
kohanso.title: Hotels & inns in Tono city
kohanso.lead: Closest lodging to Akabane slopes—~2 km from Sumata IC. Easy morning laps, then Denshoen, Anbe, and Ten-no-Yu in one folktale day.

kanko.title: Denshoen & Kaze-no-Oka area
kanko.lead: Stay near Kappa Pool and folktale exhibits at Denshoen and Michi-no-Eki Kaze-no-Oka—the core of a Tono experience beyond skiing.

station.title: Hotels near Morioka Station
station.lead: Base at Tohoku Shinkansen Morioka Station, rent a car for Tono, Kamaishi, and southern Iwate loops. Akabane ~1 hr drive—a flexible hub when snow is thin.
